source.title: Dorsal raphe serotonin neurons inhibit operant responding for reward via inputs to the ventral tegmental area but not the nucleus accumbens: evidence from studies combining optogenetic stimulation and serotonin reuptake inhibition.

confidence_rationale: Adds important nuance to MECH-187. The DRN 5-HT inhibition of operant responding requires VTA-targeted 5-HT plus an SSRI; the NAc-targeted 5-HT path is not sufficient. This relocates a major component of the 5-HT-DA interaction upstream (at VTA, the source of DA neurons) rather than downstream at the NAc target. MECH-187's NAc-locus framing needs to be qualified or extended.
failure_signatures: If MECH-187's mechanism were primarily NAc-target 5-HT signalling, optogenetic stimulation of 5-HT terminals in NAc should suffice to reduce operant responding -- it does not. The behavioural effect requires VTA-targeted 5-HT signalling.

If 5-HT and DA were straightforwardly oppositional in the operant-response axis, the SSRI requirement would not appear -- it does, suggesting the 5-HT effect is amplitude/duration-dependent rather than gating.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Optogenetic stimulation of DRN 5-HT neurons enhances downstream 5-HT release but does not by itself inhibit operant responding for sucrose reward. The behavioural inhibition requires combining the optogenetic stimulation with a low-dose SSRI (citalopram). The locus is VTA-targeted 5-HT, not NAc-targeted 5-HT.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-187 in its current statement maps the gain mechanism to the NAc target. Browne shows that, for the operant-responding axis at least, the relevant 5-HT signal is at VTA (source of DA neurons), not at NAc. This would relocate a major component of MECH-187's mechanism upstream, with VTA 5-HT setting the gain on DA cell firing rather than the NAc target setting the gain on DA-modulated EPSCs. Both could be operative; the question is which dominates.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The behavioural axis tested is operant responding for sucrose -- a particular operationalisation of motivation that may not generalise to all incentive-salience contexts MECH-187 covers. The SSRI co-requirement complicates the simple optogenetic interpretation: 5-HT release has to be both phasically driven and tonically elevated for the effect, which is a non-trivial constraint on naturalistic relevance.
mapping.source_context: Mice; optogenetic stimulation of DRN 5-HT neurons (TPH2-Cre) with selective targeting of 5-HT terminals at VTA vs NAc; SSRI co-administration; operant responding for saccharin.

confidence_components.notes: Quality work in a strong venue (Neuropsychopharmacology, Fletcher lab). Mapping fidelity is moderate -- the result complicates rather than confirms MECH-187's NAc-locus framing, in a way that is informative for the substrate question.
